intended_use: NeuroRPM is intended to quantify movement disorder symptoms during wake periods in adult patients 46 to 85 years of age with Parkinson's disease. These symptoms include tremor, bradykinesia, and dyski
intended_use_full: NeuroRPM is intended to quantify movement disorder symptoms during wake periods in adult patients 46 to 85 years of age with Parkinson's disease. These symptoms include tremor, bradykinesia, and dyskinesia. NeuroRPM is intended for clinic and home environments.
target_condition: Parkinson's disease (motor symptoms: tremor, bradykinesia, dyskinesia)
ground_truth: Unified Parkinson Disease Rating Scale (UPDRS) and Abnormal Involuntary Movement Scale (AIMS); ground truth derived from majority score of expert rater panel of 3 board-certified movement disorder specialists
other_metrics: 95% confidence intervals estimated using subject cluster bootstrap method; analysis based on events (170 tremor events, 203 bradykinesia events, 73 dyskinesia events for sensitivity; 325 tremor events, 292 bradykinesia events, 422 dyskinesia events for specificity)
